nodes.label: AI as collaborative debugger
nodes.grounding_span: I would start by providing the AI all the context I have

nodes.label: AI as implementation tool for known tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I would give the task to AI, along with context, which saves me time implementing it myself

nodes.label: AI as brainstorming partner
nodes.grounding_span: I would assign AI a task to brainstorm other ways it could be achieved

nodes.label: Human-led creative architecture and planning
nodes.grounding_span: A certain task I would prefer to handle myself would be code architecture or code planning

nodes.label: AI delegation for low-risk tasks
nodes.grounding_span: For low-risk tasks, such as tests, I tend to delegate the task completely to the AI

nodes.label: AI collaboration for high-risk tasks
nodes.grounding_span: For high-risk tasks, I tend to collaborate much more with the AI to make sure it's correct

nodes.label: AI as first point of contact
nodes.label_negative: Colleagues as first point of contact
nodes.grounding_span: AI is the go-to person

nodes.label: AI as unblocker from mental loops
nodes.grounding_span: being 'unblocked' by the AI, to make progress without being stuck for a longer time

nodes.label: Maintaining enjoyable skills despite AI capability
nodes.label_negative: Letting AI take over enjoyable skills
nodes.grounding_span: I don't see any reason not to maintain the skills I have in certain areas, as long as I enjoy them

nodes.label: AI as director of agents
nodes.grounding_span: me becoming more of a 'director of agents', rather than being hands-on writing code myself

nodes.label: Productivity and time savings
